verses.latin_vulgate: [In principio erat Verbum, et Verbum erat apud Deum, et Deus erat Verbum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ἀρχῇ ἦν ὁ λόγος καὶ ὁ λόγος ἦν πρὸς τὸν θεόν καὶ θεὸς ἦν ὁ λόγος
verses.king_james: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc erat in principio apud Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος ἦν ἐν ἀρχῇ πρὸς τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: The same was in the beginning with God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia per ipsum facta sunt: et sine ipso factum est nihil, quod factum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα δι᾽ αὐτοῦ ἐγένετο καὶ χωρὶς αὐτοῦ ἐγένετο οὐδὲ ἕν ὃ γέγονεν
verses.king_james: All things were made by him; and without him was not any thing made that was made.

verses.latin_vulgate: In ipso vita erat, et vita erat lux hominum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν αὐτῷ ζωὴ ἦν καὶ ἡ ζωὴ ἦν τὸ φῶς τῶν ἀνθρώπων
verses.king_james: In him was life; and the life was the light of men.

verses.latin_vulgate: et lux in tenebris lucet, et tenebræ eam non comprehenderunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τὸ φῶς ἐν τῇ σκοτίᾳ φαίνει καὶ ἡ σκοτία αὐτὸ οὐ κατέλαβεν
verses.king_james: And the light shineth in darkness; and the darkness comprehended it not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fuit homo missus a Deo, cui nomen erat Joannes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο ἄνθρωπος ἀπεσταλμένος παρὰ θεοῦ ὄνομα αὐτῷ ἰωάννης
verses.king_james: There was a man sent from God, whose name *was* John.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic venit in testimonium ut testimonium perhiberet de lumine, ut omnes crederent per illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος ἦλθεν εἰς μαρτυρίαν ἵνα μαρτυρήσῃ περὶ τοῦ φωτός ἵνα πάντες πιστεύσωσιν δι᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: The same came for a witness, to bear witness of the Light, that all *men* through him might believe.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non erat ille lux, sed ut testimonium perhiberet de lumine.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἦν ἐκεῖνος τὸ φῶς ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα μαρτυρήσῃ περὶ τοῦ φωτός
verses.king_james: He was not that Light, but *was sent* to bear witness of that Light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat lux vera, quæ illuminat omnem hominem venientem in hunc mundum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν τὸ φῶς τὸ ἀληθινόν ὃ φωτίζει πάντα ἄνθρωπον ἐρχόμενον εἰς τὸν κόσμον
verses.king_james: *That* was the true Light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: In mundo erat, et mundus per ipsum factus est, et mundus eum non cognovit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ ἦν καὶ ὁ κόσμος δι᾽ αὐτοῦ ἐγένετο καὶ ὁ κόσμος αὐτὸν οὐκ ἔγνω
verses.king_james: He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: In propria venit, et sui eum non receperunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς τὰ ἴδια ἦλθεν καὶ οἱ ἴδιοι αὐτὸν οὐ παρέλαβον
verses.king_james: He came unto his own, and his own received him not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quotquot autem receperunt eum, dedit eis potestatem filios Dei fieri, his qui credunt in nomine ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅσοι δὲ ἔλαβον αὐτόν ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς ἐξουσίαν τέκνα θεοῦ γενέσθαι τοῖς πιστεύουσιν εἰς τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the sons of God, *even* to them that believe on his name:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui non ex sanguinibus, neque ex voluntate carnis, neque ex voluntate viri, sed ex Deo nati sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἳ οὐκ ἐξ αἱμάτων οὐδὲ ἐκ θελήματος σαρκὸς οὐδὲ ἐκ θελήματος ἀνδρὸς ἀλλ᾽ ἐκ θεοῦ ἐγεννήθησαν
verses.king_james: Which were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et Verbum caro factum est, et habitavit in nobis: et vidimus gloriam ejus, gloriam quasi unigeniti a Patre plenum gratiæ et veritatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ λόγος σὰρξ ἐγένετο καὶ ἐσκήνωσεν ἐν ἡμῖν καὶ ἐθεασάμεθα τὴν δόξαν αὐτοῦ δόξαν ὡς μονογενοῦς παρὰ πατρός πλήρης χάριτος καὶ ἀληθείας
verses.king_james: And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Joannes testimonium perhibet de ipso, et clamat dicens: Hic erat quem dixi: Qui post me venturus est, ante me factus est: quia prior me erat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰωάννης μαρτυρεῖ περὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ κέκραγεν λέγων, οὗτος ἦν ὃν εἶπον ὁ ὀπίσω μου ἐρχόμενος ἔμπροσθέν μου γέγονεν ὅτι πρῶτός μου ἦν
verses.king_james: John bare witness of him, and cried, saying, This was he of whom I spake, He that cometh after me is preferred before me: for he was before me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et de plenitudine ejus nos omnes accepimus, et gratiam pro gratia:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκ τοῦ πληρώματος αὐτοῦ ἡμεῖς πάντες ἐλάβομεν καὶ χάριν ἀντὶ χάριτος
verses.king_james: And of his fulness have all we received, and grace for grace.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia lex per Moysen data est, gratia et veritas per Jesum Christum facta est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ὁ νόμος διὰ μωσέως ἐδόθη ἡ χάρις καὶ ἡ ἀλήθεια διὰ ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ ἐγένετο
verses.king_james: For the law was given by Moses, *but* grace and truth came by Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deum nemo vidit umquam: unigenitus Filius, qui est in sinu Patris, ipse enarravit.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: θεὸν οὐδεὶς ἑώρακεν πώποτε ὁ μονογενὴς υἱός, ὁ ὢν εἰς τὸν κόλπον τοῦ πατρὸς ἐκεῖνος ἐξηγήσατο
verses.king_james: No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared *him*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hoc est testimonium Joannis, quando miserunt Judæi ab Jerosolymis sacerdotes et Levitas ad eum ut interrogarent eum: Tu quis es?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὕτη ἐστὶν ἡ μαρτυρία τοῦ ἰωάννου ὅτε ἀπέστειλαν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι ἐξ ἱεροσολύμων ἱερεῖς καὶ λευίτας ἵνα ἐρωτήσωσιν αὐτὸν σὺ τίς εἶ
verses.king_james: And this is the record of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, Who art thou?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et confessus est, et non negavit, et confessus est: Quia non sum ego Christus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὡμολόγησεν καὶ οὐκ ἠρνήσατο καὶ ὡμολόγησεν ὅτι οὐκ εἰμὶ ἐγὼ ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: And he confessed, and denied not; but confessed, I am not the Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et interrogaverunt eum: Quid ergo? Elias es tu? Et dixit: Non sum. Propheta es tu? Et respondit: Non.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἠρώτησαν αὐτόν τί οὖν ἠλίας εἶ σύ καὶ λέγει οὐκ εἰμί ὁ προφήτης εἶ σύ καὶ ἀπεκρίθη οὔ
verses.king_james: And they asked him, What then? Art thou Elias? And he saith, I am not. Art thou that prophet? And he answered, No.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt ergo ei: Quis es ut responsum demus his qui miserunt nos? quid dicis de teipso?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον οὖν αὐτῷ τίς εἶ ἵνα ἀπόκρισιν δῶμεν τοῖς πέμψασιν ἡμᾶς τί λέγεις περὶ σεαυτοῦ
verses.king_james: Then said they unto him, Who art thou? that we may give an answer to them that sent us. What sayest thou of thyself?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait: Ego vox clamantis in deserto: Dirigite viam Domini, sicut dixit Isaias propheta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔφη ἐγὼ φωνὴ βοῶντος ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ εὐθύνατε τὴν ὁδὸν κυρίου καθὼς εἶπεν ἠσαΐας ὁ προφήτης
verses.king_james: He said, I *am* the voice of one crying in the wilderness, Make straight the way of the Lord, as said the prophet Esaias.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui missi fuerant, erant ex pharisæis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ ἀπεσταλμένοι ἦσαν ἐκ τῶν φαρισαίων
verses.king_james: And they which were sent were of the Pharisees.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et interrogaverunt eum, et dixerunt ei: Quid ergo baptizas, si tu non es Christus, neque Elias, neque propheta?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἠρώτησαν αὐτὸν καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ τί οὖν βαπτίζεις εἰ σὺ οὐκ εἶ ὁ χριστὸς οὔτε ἠλίας οὔτε ὁ προφήτης
verses.king_james: And they asked him, and said unto him, Why baptizest thou then, if thou be not that Christ, nor Elias, neither that prophet?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit eis Joannes, dicens: Ego baptizo in aqua: medius autem vestrum stetit, quem vos nescitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰωάννης λέγων, ἐγὼ βαπτίζω ἐν ὕδατι μέσος δὲ ὑμῶν ἕστηκεν ὃν ὑμεῖς οὐκ οἴδατε
verses.king_james: John answered them, saying, I baptize with water: but there standeth one among you, whom ye know not;

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse est qui post me venturus est, qui ante me factus est: cujus ego non sum dignus ut solvam ejus corrigiam calceamenti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτός ἐστιν ὁ ὀπίσω μου ἐρχόμενος ὃς ἔμπροσθέν μου γέγονεν οὗ ἐγὼ οὐκ εἰμὶ ἄξιος ἵνα λύσω αὐτοῦ τὸν ἱμάντα τοῦ ὑποδήματος
verses.king_james: He it is, who coming after me is preferred before me, whose shoe's latchet I am not worthy to unloose.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc in Bethania facta sunt trans Jordanem, ubi erat Joannes baptizans.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα ἐν βηθαβαρᾶ ἐγένετο πέραν τοῦ ἰορδάνου ὅπου ἦν ἰωάννης βαπτίζων
verses.king_james: These things were done in Bethabara beyond Jordan, where John was baptizing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Altera die vidit Joannes Jesum venientem ad se, et ait: Ecce agnus Dei, ecce qui tollit peccatum mundi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ ἐπαύριον βλέπει ὁ ἰωάννης τὸν ἰησοῦν ἐρχόμενον πρὸς αὐτόν καὶ λέγει ἴδε ὁ ἀμνὸς τοῦ θεοῦ ὁ αἴρων τὴν ἁμαρτίαν τοῦ κόσμου
verses.king_james: The next day John seeth Jesus coming unto him, and saith, Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic est de quo dixi: Post me venit vir qui ante me factus est: quia prior me erat:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτός ἐστιν περὶ οὗ ἐγὼ εἶπον ὀπίσω μου ἔρχεται ἀνὴρ ὃς ἔμπροσθέν μου γέγονεν ὅτι πρῶτός μου ἦν
verses.king_james: This is he of whom I said, After me cometh a man which is preferred before me: for he was before me.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ego nesciebam eum, sed ut manifestetur in Israël, propterea veni ego in aqua baptizans.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀγὼ οὐκ ᾔδειν αὐτόν ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα φανερωθῇ τῷ ἰσραὴλ διὰ τοῦτο ἦλθον ἐγὼ ἐν τῷ ὕδατι βαπτίζων
verses.king_james: And I knew him not: but that he should be made manifest to Israel, therefore am I come baptizing with water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et testimonium perhibuit Joannes, dicens: Quia vidi Spiritum descendentem quasi columbam de cælo, et mansit super eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐμαρτύρησεν ἰωάννης λέγων ὅτι τεθέαμαι τὸ πνεῦμα καταβαῖνον ὡσεὶ περιστερὰν ἐξ οὐρανοῦ καὶ ἔμεινεν ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And John bare record, saying, I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it abode upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego nesciebam eum: sed qui misit me baptizare in aqua, ille mihi dixit: Super quem videris Spiritum descendentem, et manentem super eum, hic est qui baptizat in Spiritu Sancto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀγὼ οὐκ ᾔδειν αὐτόν ἀλλ᾽ ὁ πέμψας με βαπτίζειν ἐν ὕδατι ἐκεῖνός μοι εἶπεν ἐφ ὃν ἂν ἴδῃς τὸ πνεῦμα καταβαῖνον καὶ μένον ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ βαπτίζων ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ
verses.king_james: And I knew him not: but he that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego vidi: et testimonium perhibui quia hic est Filius Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀγὼ ἑώρακα καὶ μεμαρτύρηκα ὅτι οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And I saw, and bare record that this is the Son of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Altera die iterum stabat Joannes, et ex discipulis ejus duo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ ἐπαύριον πάλιν εἱστήκει ὁ ἰωάννης καὶ ἐκ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ δύο
verses.king_james: Again the next day after John stood, and two of his disciples;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respiciens Jesum ambulantem, dicit: Ecce agnus Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐμβλέψας τῷ ἰησοῦ περιπατοῦντι λέγει ἴδε ὁ ἀμνὸς τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And looking upon Jesus as he walked, he saith, Behold the Lamb of God!

verses.latin_vulgate: Et audierunt eum duo discipuli loquentem, et secuti sunt Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤκουσαν αὐτοῦ οἱ δύο μαθηταὶ λαλοῦντος καὶ ἠκολούθησαν τῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: And the two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conversus autem Jesus, et videns eos sequentes se, dicit eis: Quid quæritis? Qui dixerunt ei: Rabbi (quod dicitur interpretatum Magister), ubi habitas?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: στραφεὶς δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ θεασάμενος αὐτοὺς ἀκολουθοῦντας λέγει αὐτοῖς τί ζητεῖτε οἱ δὲ εἶπον αὐτῷ ῥαββί ὃ λέγεται ἑρμηνευόμενον, διδάσκαλε ποῦ μένεις
verses.king_james: Then Jesus turned, and saw them following, and saith unto them, What seek ye? They said unto him, Rabbi, (which is to say, being interpreted, Master,) where dwellest thou?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit eis: Venite et videte. Venerunt, et viderunt ubi maneret, et apud eum manserunt die illo: hora autem erat quasi decima.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτοῖς ἔρχεσθε καὶ ἴδετε ἦλθον καὶ εἶδον ποῦ μένει καὶ παρ᾽ αὐτῷ ἔμειναν τὴν ἡμέραν ἐκείνην ὥρα δέ ἦν ὡς δεκάτη
verses.king_james: He saith unto them, Come and see. They came and saw where he dwelt, and abode with him that day: for it was about the tenth hour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem Andreas, frater Simonis Petri, unus ex duobus qui audierant a Joanne, et secuti fuerant eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν ἀνδρέας ὁ ἀδελφὸς σίμωνος πέτρου εἷς ἐκ τῶν δύο τῶν ἀκουσάντων παρὰ ἰωάννου καὶ ἀκολουθησάντων αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: One of the two which heard John *speak*, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Invenit hic primum fratrem suum Simonem, et dicit ei: Invenimus Messiam (quod est interpretatum Christus).
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὑρίσκει οὗτος πρῶτος τὸν ἀδελφὸν τὸν ἴδιον σίμωνα καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ εὑρήκαμεν τὸν μεσσίαν ὅ ἐστιν μεθερμηνευόμενον ὅ χριστός
verses.king_james: He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et adduxit eum ad Jesum. Intuitus autem eum Jesus, dixit: Tu es Simon, filius Jona; tu vocaberis Cephas, quod interpretatur Petrus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤγαγεν αὐτὸν πρὸς τὸν ἰησοῦν ἐμβλέψας δὲ αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν σὺ εἶ σίμων ὁ υἱὸς ἰωνᾶ σὺ κληθήσῃ κηφᾶς ὃ ἑρμηνεύεται πέτρος
verses.king_james: And he brought him to Jesus. And when Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone.

verses.latin_vulgate: In crastinam voluit exire in Galilæam, et invenit Philippum. Et dicit ei Jesus: Sequere me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ ἐπαύριον ἠθέλησεν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐξελθεῖν εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν καὶ εὑρίσκει φίλιππον καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ἀκολούθει μοι
verses.king_james: The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee, and findeth Philip, and saith unto him, Follow me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem Philippus a Bethsaida, civitate Andreæ et Petri.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ὁ φίλιππος ἀπὸ βηθσαϊδά ἐκ τῆς πόλεως ἀνδρέου καὶ πέτρου
verses.king_james: Now Philip was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Invenit Philippus Nathanaël, et dicit ei: Quem scripsit Moyses in lege, et prophetæ, invenimus Jesum filium Joseph a Nazareth.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὑρίσκει φίλιππος τὸν ναθαναὴλ καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ὅν ἔγραψεν μωσῆς ἐν τῷ νόμῳ καὶ οἱ προφῆται εὑρήκαμεν ἰησοῦν τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἰωσὴφ τὸν ἀπὸ ναζαρέτ
verses.king_james: Philip findeth Nathanael, and saith unto him, We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, did write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit ei Nathanaël: A Nazareth potest aliquid boni esse? Dicit ei Philippus: Veni et vide.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ναθαναήλ ἐκ ναζαρὲτ δύναταί τι ἀγαθὸν εἶναι λέγει αὐτῷ φίλιππος ἔρχου καὶ ἴδε
verses.king_james: And Nathanael said unto him, Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth? Philip saith unto him, Come and see.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidit Jesus Nathanaël venientem ad se, et dicit de eo: Ecce vere Israëlita, in quo dolus non est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶδεν ὁ ἰησοῦς τὸν ναθαναὴλ ἐρχόμενον πρὸς αὐτὸν καὶ λέγει περὶ αὐτοῦ ἴδε ἀληθῶς ἰσραηλίτης ἐν ᾧ δόλος οὐκ ἔστιν
verses.king_james: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and saith of him, Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile!

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Nathanaël: Unde me nosti? Respondit Jesus, et dixit ei: Priusquam te Philippus vocavit, cum esses sub ficu, vidi te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ναθαναήλ πόθεν με γινώσκεις ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ πρὸ τοῦ σε φίλιππον φωνῆσαι ὄντα ὑπὸ τὴν συκῆν εἶδόν σε
verses.king_james: Nathanael saith unto him, Whence knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said unto him, Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit ei Nathanaël, et ait: Rabbi, tu es Filius Dei, tu es rex Israël.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ναθαναήλ καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ῥαββί σὺ εἶ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ σὺ εἶ ὁ βασιλεὺς τοῦ ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: Nathanael answered and saith unto him, Rabbi, thou art the Son of God; thou art the King of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus, et dixit ei: Quia dixi tibi: Vidi te sub ficu, credis; majus his videbis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὅτι εἶπόν σοι εἶδόν σε ὑποκάτω τῆς συκῆς πιστεύεις μείζω τούτων ὄψει
verses.king_james: Jesus answered and said unto him, Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicit ei: Amen, amen dico vobis, videbitis cælum apertum, et angelos Dei ascendentes, et descendentes supra Filium hominis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ἀπ᾽ ἄρτι ὄψεσθε τὸν οὐρανὸν ἀνεῳγότα καὶ τοὺς ἀγγέλους τοῦ θεοῦ ἀναβαίνοντας καὶ καταβαίνοντας ἐπὶ τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου
verses.king_james: And he saith unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.
